primer.markdown:
# From zero to a Claude reply in three lines of shell

Before an agent can loop, stream, or call tools, it has to *exist*. The Claude Agent SDK compresses that first-existence step into a very small ritual: install one package, export one key, run one message. The vendored multiplatform skill lays out the exact commands in its README — one block per language (`npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk`, `pip install anthropic`, `go get github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-go`), then a twin block that calls `client.messages.create({ model, max_tokens, messages })` and prints the reply. That is the whole scaffold.

Two boundaries matter here. The first is the **API key boundary**: the SDK reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from the process environment, never from a hardcoded string. Put the key in `.env`, load it with `dotenv`, and your scaffold is safe to push. The second is the **model boundary**: you pass a model ID (the skill's canonical default is `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`) and a `max_tokens` cap. Those two values are the knobs for cost and latency for every subsequent module in this Quest.

What the scaffold intentionally does *not* give you yet: a system prompt, a tool, a stream, a loop. All it gives you is a round-trip. Once that round-trip works — once `console.log(message.content)` prints actual Claude output — you have the bedrock every later module builds on. Skip this and nothing else compiles.
primer.key_insight: An agent scaffold is three things: a package, a key in the environment, and one `messages.create` call. Everything else in this Quest is an elaboration of that round-trip.

visual.alt: A three-stage pipeline: stage 1 is a terminal running `npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk`, stage 2 is a .env file holding `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...`, stage 3 is a node process emitting `await client.messages.create({...})` which round-trips with the Anthropic Messages API and prints `message.content` to stdout.
visual.alt_es: Una tuberia de tres etapas: etapa 1 es una terminal ejecutando `npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk`, etapa 2 es un archivo .env que guarda `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...`, etapa 3 es un proceso node llamando `await client.messages.create({...})` que hace un round-trip con la Messages API de Anthropic e imprime `message.content` en stdout.

sandbox_policy.csp: default-src 'none'; style-src 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; script-src 'unsafe-inline'; img-src data:; font-src https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'self'

interactive.success_criterion: Learner reads the annotated scaffold block, can identify which line installs the SDK, which line reads the API key from the environment, and which line performs the round-trip call.

questions.prompt: In the First Message TypeScript block (README.md lines 75-91), where does the `Anthropic` client read the API key from?
options: From a hardcoded string literal inside `new Anthropic({...})`
questions.explanation: README.md line 81: `apiKey: process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. The SDK reads the key from the process environment on construction. This is the boundary that keeps secrets out of source control.

questions.prompt: Which field in `client.messages.create({...})` is the upper bound on Claude's reply length?
questions.explanation: README.md line 86: `max_tokens: 1024`. `model` picks the model, `messages` is the conversation, and `max_tokens` caps the reply. Every subsequent module in this Quest inherits this cap.

questions.prompt: The README.md Installation block (lines 59-72) lists one-line installs for three languages. Which command installs the TypeScript SDK?
questions.explanation: README.md line 61: `npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk` installs the TypeScript/Node SDK. `pip install anthropic` (line 66) is Python and `go get ...` (line 71) is Go. All three scaffold the same round-trip — pick the one your stack already speaks.
